items.title: Korea’s AI-Heavy Market Now Sets the Tone for Global Stocks
items.description: For fund managers in London, New York and Tokyo, a new ritual has emerged before trading gets underway: checking South Korean stocks.

items.title: Vanishing CLO Profits Are Sparking Infighting: Credit Weekly
items.description: A type of investment that once generated some of Wall Street’s juiciest fixed-income returns has deteriorated so badly that investors are heading for the exits and arguing about who’s to blame.

items.title: Zelenskyy May Fire Commander Syrskyi After Protests, FT Reports
items.description: Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is considering firing Armed Forces commander-in-chief Oleksandr Syrskyi, the Financial Times reported, following days of turmoil engulfing the country’s military

items.title: Not Done Yet Refunding Tariffs or Realizing AI’s Promise
items.description: The markets continue to be dominated by news of artificial intelligence

items.title: ICICI Bank’s First-Quarter Profit Beats Estimate as Loans Surge
items.description: ICICI Bank Ltd. posted better-than-expected results as India’s second-largest private-sector lender benefited from healthy loan growth across retail and corporate segments.

items.title: Kuwait Energy Infrastructure Hit During Intense Iran Attacks
items.description: Kuwait suffered one of its worst nights of Iranian retaliatory attacks since the Middle East conflict began, with strikes on an oil facility and a second power plant hit in as many days.

items.title: Hungary Crackdown on $20 Billion EV Sector Puts China on Notice

items.title: China’s Siemens Competitor Eyes Buying Its Way Into Europe
items.description: Shenzhen Inovance Technology Co. Ltd. is considering acquisitions in Europe, signaling the Chinese company is readying a push to compete in Europe with industrial incumbents like Siemens AG and top au

items.title: India Moves Against Youth Protesters Before Parliament Session
items.description: Indian authorities moved against protesters in New Delhi ahead of demonstrations against unemployment and alleged examination scams, as the movement adds pressure on Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s gov

items.title: China Solar Cell Exports Extend Decline in June
items.description: China’s solar cell exports fell for a second month in June, an indication that overseas demand is weakening after a surge in shipments earlier in the year.

items.title: China Seeks Stable, Transparent Mineral Rules From Indonesia
items.description: China said it hopes Indonesia can provide a stable and transparent policy environment for its mineral sector, as the two countries deepen economic ties.
